title: Morse code messaging on Heltec Meshpocket

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: I asked Claude AI to develop a way to send message sans phone app with the Heltec Meshpocket and now we've got morse code messaging with the one user button. 100% vibe coded, made just for fun and because I can. Comes with channel picker pre-selection (channels obviously need to be set beforehand with the companion app, otherwise you're restricted to Public.) Can see incoming messages per channel on the morse screen. Takes a bit to get the hang of the correct timing (test is definitely the easiest word to type!) If anyone wants to have a go, it's the Meshpocket companion BLE build with max 500 contacts, max 8 channels, and 256kb offline queue set, optimised for use with 62.5khz band settings. Code and guide on how to type found here: https://github.com/pelgraine/MeshCore/blob/meshpocket-morse-port/variants/mesh_pocket/Morse_Compose_Guide.md

messages.author: G4WKW-Richard
messages.text: I absolutely love this I don't have a Heltec Meshpocket at the moment, but will certainly consider buying one, just for this functionality! It looks like you have implemented an excellent protocol for it. I am a radio amateur and know Morse Code. I presume that there is no way of receiving any audible feedback when keying though, is there?

messages.author: pelgraine
messages.text: Just to be absolutely transparent I'm not a developer and this was crafted entirely using Claude Code No, unfortunately - no buzzer at all. Not even a way to get the little led light on the front to give you feedback
messages.content_html: Just to be absolutely transparent I'm not a developer and this was crafted entirely using Claude Code No, unfortunately - no buzzer at all. Not even a way to get the little led light on the front to give you feedback

messages.author: G4WKW-Richard
messages.text: That is OK, I would suggest that if you have an Android device, you try a free app called "cwstudio". It has two types of Morse key under "handling", with the first one being a "straightkey". I have just been trying that with the sound on my phone turned right down and found that I can still key OK with no sound, so I could certainly manage (it displays each letter after you key it). (edited)
